name: Bette Midler
abstract: Bette Midler ( bet MID-lər; born December 1, 1945) is an American actress, comedian, singer, and author. Throughout her six-decade career Midler has received numerous accolades, including four Golden Globe Awards, three Grammy Awards, three Primetime Emmy Awards, two Tony Awards, and a Kennedy Center Honor, in addition to nominations for…

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Cee Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I asked J. C., what do you think of Bette Midler's recording of your "Empty Bed Blues"? And he said, "It's all right. She's doing it her way."

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Allred
about_quotes.evidence: And Bette Midler and David Hyde Pierce — it’s going to be a good show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Allred interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 24, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: When I started to get hits with Flack and Midler... His list of production credits include the names Charles Mingus, Bette Midler
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: Bette Midler I think was playing there. They were already famous, I think, and they were making these club appearances.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lorraine Feather
about_quotes.evidence: Charlotte Crossley, who was in “Jesus Christ Superstar” with me and also was a Harlette with Bette Midler
about_quotes.doc_title: Lorraine Feather interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 22, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Janis Siegel
about_quotes.evidence: I picked out like Roberta Flack’s record and Bette Midler’s first record
about_quotes.doc_title: Janis Siegel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 2015
